answer: Never deploy on Fridays after 3pm — the oncall rotation is thin and we've had 3 bad Friday incidents
Don't modify src/billing/reconcile.ts without asking Sarah — that file is the source of truth for revenue
New hires always forget to run bun run setup before starting dev — the DB won't initialise and you'll get confusing errors for hours
The migrations/legacy/001-init.sql file looks like it should be cleaned up but it's referenced by the Docker build — deleting it breaks the CI pipeline
People always try to import directly from src/server/auth.ts inside the MCP layer — that creates a circular dep. Use the public re-export in src/index.ts instead
All database queries must use parameterized SQL — no string interpolation. The linter doesn't catch this but every PR reviewer will reject it
We switched from Node.js to Bun in early 2025. Any third-party library that uses native Node addons will break — check compatibility before adding deps
src/mcp/server.ts and src/store/database.ts are the two files that touch every feature — changes there require a full test run and a second reviewer
Database migrations are manual — you must run the migration script against production yourself after deploying. The app does NOT auto-migrate on startup

The .gyst-wiki.json config file in the project root controls the DB path and token limits. If you clone the repo without it, gyst falls back to defaults but logs a warning you can safely ignore on day one
